req.title: Bulk data export (audit)
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-091 mandates three orthogonal concerns: (1) a provisioned client can successfully trigger a FHIR bulk export, receive a 202 with Content-Location, poll until complete, and retrieve NDJSON output; (2) an unauthorised caller is rejected with HTTP 403; (3) a large dataset crosses a pagination boundary so that multiple NDJSON files are emitted. Each concern maps cleanly to one heuristic: EP covers the happy-path authorised request, NEG covers the 403 rejection, and BVA targets the exact page-boundary where the implementation must split output into a second NDJSON chunk. An additional STC test verifies the full job state machine (queued -> in-progress -> completed) and AuditEvent emission at each transition, satisfying the end-to-end audit requirement. All tests use placeholder fixtures and helpers to stay within the 40-line limit while remaining runnable once those fixtures are wired.

tests.name: test_bulk_export_authorised_client_returns_202_and_ndjson
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client is provisioned with valid bulk-export scope
At least one FHIR resource exists in the server
actions: Poll Content-Location until status is complete
Download each output URL and verify NDJSON format
expected: Polling endpoint eventually returns 200 with output array
Each output file is valid NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
AuditEvent recorded for the export initiation
trace: R-HLT-091: provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export
R-HLT-091: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_bulk_export_authorised_client_returns_202_and_ndjson(
    authorized_bulk_client,
    fhir_base_url,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    resp = authorized_bulk_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    poll_url = resp.headers.get("Content-Location")
    assert poll_url, "Content-Location header missing"

    status_body = poll_until_complete(authorized_bulk_client, poll_url, timeout=30)

    assert status_body.get("output"), "No output files in completed export"
    for entry in status_body["output"]:
        assert entry["type"] in ("Patient", "Observation", "Encounter")
        ndjson_resp = authorized_bulk_client.get(entry["url"])
        assert ndjson_resp.status_code == 200
        lines = ndjson_resp.text.strip().splitlines()
        assert lines, "NDJSON file is empty"
        for line in lines:
            row = parse_json_line(line)
            assert "resourceType" in row

    events = audit_log_reader.fetch_events(operation="bulk-export")
    assert any(e["action"] == "E" for e in events), "No AuditEvent found for export"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_unauthorised_client_receives_403
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client presents an expired or missing Bearer token
actions: GET /$export with an invalid / missing Authorization header
expected: Response body contains FHIR OperationOutcome with severity error
No export job is queued on the server
AuditEvent recorded with outcome code indicating failure
trace: R-HLT-091: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_export_unauthorised_client_receives_403(
    fhir_base_url,
    http_session_no_auth,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    resp = http_session_no_auth.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i["severity"] == "error" for i in issues)

    jobs = list_active_export_jobs(fhir_base_url)
    assert len(jobs) == 0, "Export job must not be queued for unauthorised request"

    events = audit_log_reader.fetch_events(operation="bulk-export", outcome="8")
    assert events, "AuditEvent with failure outcome not recorded for 403 rejection"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_at_page_boundary_produces_two_ndjson_files
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client is provisioned with valid bulk-export scope
Server page size is configured to PAGE_SIZE (e.g. 1000 resources)
Exactly PAGE_SIZE + 1 resources of the same type exist so the split is forced
actions: Seed the server with PAGE_SIZE + 1 Patient resources via seed_resources fixture
Count output files for type Patient
expected: Completed status body contains exactly 2 output entries for Patient
First file contains PAGE_SIZE NDJSON lines
Second file contains exactly 1 NDJSON line
AuditEvent emitted for both file chunks
trace: R-HLT-091: large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output
R-HLT-091: boundary at page-size N and N+1 resources
tests.executable:
import pytest

PAGE_SIZE = 1000

def test_bulk_export_at_page_boundary_produces_two_ndjson_files(
    authorized_bulk_client,
    fhir_base_url,
    seed_resources,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    seed_resources(resource_type="Patient", count=PAGE_SIZE + 1)

    resp = authorized_bulk_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    poll_url = resp.headers["Content-Location"]

    status_body = poll_until_complete(authorized_bulk_client, poll_url, timeout=60)

    patient_files = [e for e in status_body["output"] if e["type"] == "Patient"]
    assert len(patient_files) == 2, f"Expected 2 NDJSON files, got {len(patient_files)}"

    lines_0 = download_ndjson_lines(authorized_bulk_client, patient_files[0]["url"])
    lines_1 = download_ndjson_lines(authorized_bulk_client, patient_files[1]["url"])
    assert len(lines_0) == PAGE_SIZE
    assert len(lines_1) == 1

    events = audit_log_reader.fetch_events(operation="bulk-export")
    assert len(events) >= 2, "AuditEvent must be emitted for each output chunk"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_job_state_transitions_and_audit_trail
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client is provisioned with valid bulk-export scope
At least one FHIR resource exists
actions: GET /$export to transition job to QUEUED state
Immediately poll Content-Location and assert 202 (in-progress or queued)
Wait for final poll to return 200 (completed state)
Fetch AuditEvents and verify one exists per state transition
expected: Initial GET returns 202 – job enters QUEUED state
First poll returns 202 with X-Progress header indicating in-progress
Final poll returns 200 – job in COMPLETED state
AuditEvent entries cover QUEUED, IN-PROGRESS, and COMPLETED transitions
trace: R-HLT-091: export job queued expected outcome
R-HLT-091: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation end-to-end
tests.executable:
import time
import pytest

def test_bulk_export_job_state_transitions_and_audit_trail(
    authorized_bulk_client,
    fhir_base_url,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    init_resp = authorized_bulk_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert init_resp.status_code == 202, "Job must be queued with 202"
    poll_url = init_resp.headers["Content-Location"]

    first_poll = authorized_bulk_client.get(poll_url)
    assert first_poll.status_code in (200, 202), "Unexpected poll status"
    observed_states = {"queued"}
    if first_poll.status_code == 202:
        observed_states.add("in-progress")

    final_body = poll_until_complete(authorized_bulk_client, poll_url, timeout=30)
    observed_states.add("completed")
    assert final_body.get("output"), "Completed job must have output"

    events = audit_log_reader.fetch_events(operation="bulk-export")
    event_subtypes = {e.get("subtype", [{}])[0].get("code") for e in events}
    assert "bulk-export-kickoff" in event_subtypes
    assert "bulk-export-complete" in event_subtypes
    assert "queued" in observed_states
    assert "completed" in observed_states
